name: Gateron KS-15 Optical Switch
summary: Full-size optical switch with PCB-mounted IR sensing — the most widely available optical switch for individual purchase
pinDescription: Switch has NO electrical pins — only alignment/retention posts. Optical sensing (IR emitter + phototransistor) is mounted on the PCB. The switch housing has a light channel with a shutter on the stem.
hotswapNote: Gateron optical hot-swap design is integral — switches snap into PCB receptacles. NOT compatible with standard MX hot-swap sockets.
scanningNote: PCB must have IR LED + phototransistor pairs at each switch position. IR LED forward voltage ~1.2V with current-limiting resistor. Digital output (beam interrupted = pressed). Can be arranged in a multiplexed scanning matrix.

opticalComponents.emitter: IR LED (940nm typical), PCB-mounted, through-hole or SMD
opticalComponents.location: Both on PCB — switch housing provides light channel only

footprintNote: No official KiCad footprint. PCB design is proprietary. Must be reverse-engineered from Gateron SK61/SK64 keyboard PCBs. Requires IR LED + phototransistor footprints at each key position in addition to switch mounting holes.
designNotes: Switch has NO electrical pins — the switch is purely mechanical with a light shutter.
PCB design requires IR LED + phototransistor pairs at EVERY switch position.
This fundamentally changes the PCB design — you need optical component footprints alongside each switch mounting point.
The optical path between IR LED and phototransistor must be precisely aligned with the switch housing's light channel.
Phototransistor output is digital (beam blocked = key pressed), but can be arranged into a scanning matrix similar to a standard key matrix.
No debounce needed — optical sensing is instantaneous and bounce-free.
IR LED power draw is significant for a full keyboard (~5mA per LED × 86 keys = ~430mA continuous).
Can use multiplexed LED driving to reduce power (pulse LEDs during scanning, not continuously).
features: Contactless operation — no metal contacts, extremely long lifespan
Zero debounce time — optical sensing is instantaneous
Widely available individually on AliExpress and keyboard part vendors
Standard MX cross (+) stem — compatible with all MX keycaps
Multiple actuation distances available via variant selection (1.0mm–2.3mm)
concerns: PCB must include IR LED + phototransistor at each key — significant design complexity
No official KiCad footprint — must reverse-engineer from existing Gateron optical PCBs
NOT compatible with standard MX hot-swap sockets (uses Gateron's optical design)
PCB-mounted optical components make hand-soldering more complex
Digital only — no analog/adjustable actuation capability
IR LED power consumption is higher than diode-matrix mechanical switches
